codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly adds bank reconciliation session workflow and discrepancy resolution capabilities as specified. The BankReconciliationPage provides full session lifecycle (create/resume/update/complete) with proper state management, cleared transaction toggles, and read-only handling for completed sessions. The ReconciliationDashboardPage enhancement adds a well-designed discrepancy resolution modal supporting all three required paths (acknowledge, adjustment journal, write-off) with proper account selection gating for the latter two. All 13 new tests cover the key workflows and edge cases.

issues.description: The cleared balance calculation sums (credit - debit) which assumes credits increase bank balance and debits decrease it. This is correct for a bank account perspective, but worth documenting since accounting conventions can vary. The implementation is consistent with the expected behavior.

The discrepancy resolution modal removes the resolved item from the open list immediately after success. This provides good UX feedback but doesn't reload the full subledger report. Consider whether the subledger status cards (AR/AP) should also refresh to reflect the resolution impact.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker consistently followed the existing accounting page patterns: using formatINR helper, consistent use of CSS variables for theming, PageHeader component usage, and ToastProvider integration. The pattern of extracting form components as separate sub-components (CreateSessionForm, SessionDetailView, ResolveModal) matches the existing codebase structure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: BankReconciliationPage.tsx mirrors MonthEndChecklistPage.tsx structure; ReconciliationDashboardPage modal pattern matches existing dialog conventions

sharedStateObservations.observation: The bankReconciliationApi namespace was correctly added to accountingApi.ts following the existing pattern of namespacing related API methods (gstReconciliationApi, monthEndApi, auditApi). This keeps the API layer maintainable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: accountingApi.ts lines 1018-1095 show proper namespaced API methods matching existing patterns

sharedStateObservations.observation: The API types (BankReconciliationSessionCreateRequest, BankReconciliationSessionDetailDto, ReconciliationDiscrepancyDto, etc.) were correctly inferred from the backend contract. The types include all necessary fields for the session workflow and discrepancy resolution.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Type definitions in accountingApi.ts match the expected backend contract with proper DTOs for request/response shapes

summary: The implementation fully satisfies VAL-ACCT-009 and VAL-ACCT-010 requirements. Bank reconciliation sessions can be created, resumed, updated with cleared items, saved mid-progress, and completed into a read-only state. Discrepancies can be resolved through acknowledge, adjustment journal, or write-off paths, with account selection properly required for the latter two. The code quality is high, follows established patterns, and the 13 new tests provide good coverage of the session lifecycle and discrepancy resolution flows. All validation commands (lint, typecheck, test) pass with 1332 total tests.
